name: Meetlark MCP Server — Agent-First Meeting Polls
what_it_does: MCP server for Meetlark — an agent-first meeting scheduling poll platform. Enables AI agents to create meeting polls, collect availability votes from participants, and find optimal meeting times. Designed natively for both human and AI agent participants in scheduling workflows.
use_cases: Creating meeting availability polls from scheduling automation agents
Collecting and aggregating meeting time preferences from executive assistant agents
Finding optimal meeting slots for groups from calendar coordination agents
Automating the back-and-forth of meeting scheduling in multi-party coordination
not_for: Calendar booking with confirmed slots (polling/availability focus)
Enterprise calendar systems requiring deep calendar integration
Single-person scheduling without group coordination needs
best_when: An AI assistant manages meeting scheduling for a human and needs to poll multiple participants for availability — replacing the manual back-and-forth of finding a common time slot.
avoid_when: You need direct calendar booking integration. Meetlark is for availability polling, not confirmed reservation booking.

auth.notes: Meetlark account credentials required. Remote MCP endpoint. Polls are shareable links — no auth required for participants to vote.

pricing.notes: Emerging scheduling service — pricing not publicly documented.

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Meeting participant names and availability times are personal scheduling data
Poll links are shareable — ensure appropriate access controls on poll results
Agent-created polls must be clearly attributed — participants should know they're responding to AI
Novel service — validate that poll delivery and voting works reliably before production use
Meeting polls require participant action — humans must respond for the system to be useful
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Scheduling data includes personal availability patterns. Poll results show individual scheduling preferences. Emerging service with unverified security posture.

security_score_components.security_notes: Personal scheduling data. Shareable poll links require access control. Availability data reveals personal patterns. Emerging platform.
